text: # L10 · OOS test (summary)
- Before the test, the hash of each strategy file goes into the hash register.
- One parquet file serves both training and the OOS test — that is why it has no cuts and no features (register C-71).

- [ ] **Causality verified:** a test detecting use of any candle `> t` when computing a feature/line for `t` (e.g. shifting the future does not change historical values).
- [ ] OOS one-shot: artifacts frozen before the test; the OOS result never returns to tuning.

- **ML pipeline** — the whole flow from raw market data to the strategy file and the OOS test; 10 levels L1–L10 in the visualization.

- **Causality / zero look-ahead** — the overriding rule: for candle `t` we use only data from candles **≤ t**. No feature, line or decision depends on the future (the only exception: the explicit label window counted for
- **One-shot** — we test the OOS window **once**, after freezing the artifacts; the OOS result never goes back into tuning.
- **OOS** (`2024-01-02 → 2026-05-29`) — **frozen**: one test run; zero tuning after looking at the results.
- **boundary assertion** — an automatic test: no `[t0, t0+H]` window crosses a window boundary (DoD F4).
## L10 · OOS test: 503 assets × metrics
- **OOS test** — one run over the OOS window (`2024-01-02 → 2026-05-29`).
- **hash registry** — the hash of each artifact recorded **before** the test; from that moment the files are immutable.
